text_content: HAL 4.0: An additional CNSC (Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission) application must be developed and submitted close to the time of the Health Canada submission, per SOR/200-205, Section 10 of the Nuclear Safety and Control Act; section 1 of this act presents Definitions (“certificate”, “Class II prescribed equipment”, etc.). Anticipated time to receive the necessary certificate is approximately 12 months. The Varian Palo Alto Radiation Safety group develops and submits the CNSC application.The CNSC application package is sent immediately after the Health Canada Medical Device license submission package is sent. The documents required are taken from the ISD, with additional documents developed by the Varian Palo Alto Radiation Safety group. title: 8. General & Specific Regulatory Requirements
text_content: For the complete list of applicable standards, related deliverables and organizations responsible see D47457 HAL 4.0 Applicable Standards  [Reference 1]. HAL shall comply with the listed standards and regulations and evidence in the form of test reports or checklists as defined in the Applicable Product Standards document must be provided. It is the responsibility of the project team to ensure that regulatory requirements, including national language requirements are included within design and development plans , according to cGMPs and national legislation. Regulatory submissions may require the product to meet additional specific standards recognized by individual national governmental authorities. Where these standards have been identified, they are listed in the country-specific subsections within Section 8 of this Regulatory Plan. text_content: Justification regarding the presence of CMR and/or endocrine-disrupting substances The justification for the presence of such substances shall be based upon: a) an analysis and estimation of potential patient or user exposure to the substance; b) an analysis of possible alternative substances, materials or designs, including, where available, information about independent research, peer-reviewed studies, scientific opinions from relevant scientific committees and an analysis of the availability of such alternatives; c) argumentation as to why possible substance and/ or material substitutes, if available, or design changes, if feasible, are inappropriate N/A HAL does not contain CMR or endocrine disrupting substances.
